29.reason: [Error: When data == root->data, the node is not inserted, violating the BST property that right child should be >= root. According to the problem, values >= root should go to the right subtree. The condition should be `else` (or `else if(data >= root->data)`) to handle equal values.]

26.reason: This condition should be `else` (i.e., data >= root->data) to match the problem description where right child values are greater than or equal to the root. Using `data > root->data` will ignore equal values, causing them not to be inserted.

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 1.0 , reason:  The problem specifies that the right child's value should be greater than or equal to the root's value. Using '>' ignores duplicate values, which should be inserted into the right subtree.

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 1.0 , reason:  [The problem requires right child to be >= root, but this code only inserts when data > root->data, ignoring equal values. This causes duplicate values to be silently dropped, leading to incorrect tree structure and missing leaf nodes.]
